text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

PATIENT: Raymond Kowalski
MRN: [REDACTED]
ACCESSION NUMBER: SP-[REDACTED]
ATTENDING UROLOGIST: [REDACTED]
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST: [REDACTED]
CLINICAL SERVICE: Urology / Urologic Oncology

CLINICAL HISTORY:
71-year-old male (age 67 at time of procedure) presenting with markedly elevated serum PSA of 187 ng/mL detected on routine screening. Digital rectal examination notable for diffusely hard, nodular prostate. Pelvic MRI (PI-RADS 5) demonstrated a large dominant lesion in the peripheral and transition zone with bilateral extraprostatic extension and bilateral seminal vesicle invasion (cT3b). Bilateral pelvic lymphadenopathy and multiple bone lesions on staging CT and bone scan. Transrectal ultrasound-guided (TRUS) biopsy performed for tissue diagnosis.

SPECIMEN IDENTIFICATION:
Specimen A: Prostate needle biopsy cores, 12 cores submitted in 6 separate cassettes as follows:
  - Cassette A1: Right apex (2 cores)
  - Cassette A2: Right mid (2 cores)
  - Cassette A3: Right base (2 cores)
  - Cassette A4: Left apex (2 cores)
  - Cassette A5: Left mid (2 cores)
  - Cassette A6: Left base (2 cores)

TYPE OF PROCEDURE:
Transrectal ultrasound-guided (TRUS) prostate needle biopsy, 12-core systematic biopsy protocol

----------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION:
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
Received in formalin are 12 cylindrical needle biopsy cores submitted in 6 labeled cassettes corresponding to anatomic sextant locations. Each core measures 1.2 to 1.5 cm in length and 0.1 to 0.15 cm in diameter. Cores are tan-pink to white-grey and firm. All cores are entirely submitted after inking and embedding in a per-cassette fashion. Representative sections are taken at 3 levels per cassette. All tissue is submitted.

Total tissue length submitted: approximately 16.3 cm aggregate across 12 cores.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
All 12 biopsy cores are involved by malignancy. Core involvement ranges from 60% to 95% of core length (see table below). The neoplastic glands are arranged in poorly formed, fused, and solid sheet-like patterns without luminal differentiation, consistent with Gleason pattern 5 (primary pattern). Areas exhibiting cribriform architecture with central necrosis (comedonecrosis) are also identified. A secondary component of back-to-back poorly formed and fused glands without well-defined individual glandular lumina is consistent with Gleason pattern 4.

Gleason Score: 5 + 4 = 9 (Grade Group 5)

Summary of core involvement:
  - A1 (Right apex, core 1): 85% involvement, Gleason 5+4=9
  - A1 (Right apex, core 2): 90% involvement, Gleason 5+4=9
  - A2 (Right mid, core 1): 80% involvement, Gleason 5+4=9
  - A2 (Right mid, core 2): 95% involvement, Gleason 5+5=10
  - A3 (Right base, core 1): 75% involvement, Gleason 5+4=9
  - A3 (Right base, core 2): 70% involvement, Gleason 5+4=9
  - A4 (Left apex, core 1): 85% involvement, Gleason 5+4=9
  - A4 (Left apex, core 2): 60% involvement, Gleason 5+4=9
  - A5 (Left mid, core 1): 90% involvement, Gleason 5+4=9
  - A5 (Left mid, core 2): 75% involvement, Gleason 5+4=9
  - A6 (Left base, core 1): 80% involvement, Gleason 5+4=9
  - A6 (Left base, core 2): 65% involvement, Gleason 5+4=9

Total: 12 of 12 cores positive (100%)

PERINEURAL INVASION: Present. Identified in 8 of 12 cores. Extensive perineural invasion noted in right mid and bilateral apex cores, with tumor wrapping circumferentially around large-caliber nerves.

LYMPHOVASCULAR INVASION: Suspicious foci present; lymphovascular spaces adjacent to tumor nests are identified in right mid and left mid cores. Cannot be definitively excluded. Additional tissue level sections reviewed; confirmed as suspicious/present.

INTRADUCTAL CARCINOMA OF THE PROSTATE (IDC-P): Extensive intraductal carcinoma component identified, characterized by expansile cribriform nests filling pre-existing prostatic ductal and acinar spaces with preservation of the basal cell layer (confirmed by p63 and CK5/6 staining; see ancillary studies). IDC-P is present in 7 of 12 cores and comprises a substantial volume of the tumor. This finding is associated with high-grade aggressive pathology and is consistent with the degree of PSA elevation.

NEUROENDOCRINE DIFFERENTIATION: No morphologic evidence of small cell carcinoma or pure neuroendocrine carcinoma. Synaptophysin and chromogranin immunostains are negative (see ancillary studies). No rosette formation, salt-and-pepper chromatin, or scant cytoplasm characteristic of high-grade neuroendocrine carcinoma identified.

ADDITIONAL HISTOLOGIC FEATURES:
- High-grade cytologic atypia is present throughout: prominent nucleoli, vesicular chromatin, and moderate to abundant pale cytoplasm.
- Stromal desmoplasia present, focally prominent in right base cores.
- No granulomatous inflammation.
- No evidence of high-grade PIN (masked by invasive carcinoma).
- Benign prostatic tissue present only at the extreme distal ends of the least-involved cores.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES:
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed in-house):

1. PSA (polyclonal): POSITIVE — diffuse, strong cytoplasmic and nuclear rim positivity in the majority of neoplastic glands, confirming prostatic origin.

2. PSMA (prostate-specific membrane antigen): POSITIVE — moderate to strong, diffuse membranous positivity in neoplastic cells, supporting prostatic origin.

3. Androgen Receptor (AR): POSITIVE — strong, diffuse nuclear positivity in neoplastic cells (H-score approximately 280/300). Intensity uniform across cores. This finding indicates high AR expression and supports hormone-driven tumor biology.

4. NKX3.1: POSITIVE — strong, diffuse nuclear positivity, corroborating prostatic lineage.

5. ERG (by IHC, clone EPR3864): NEGATIVE — no nuclear ERG expression in neoplastic cells. This result indicates absence of TMPRSS2-ERG gene fusion by IHC surrogate (consistent with negative ERG FISH, if performed).

6. PTEN (by IHC, clone 138G6): LOSS OF EXPRESSION — heterogeneous loss of PTEN protein expression observed. A subset of neoplastic gland-containing areas demonstrates complete absence of PTEN immunostaining while retaining internal stromal control staining, indicative of PTEN deletion/inactivation in the tumor. This finding is heterogeneous, with complete loss in approximately 70% of tumor-containing cores and retained (though reduced) staining in the remainder.

7. Ki-67 (MIB-1): APPROXIMATELY 35% — high proliferative index. Positive nuclei identified in approximately 35% of neoplastic cells on representative hotspot sections (right mid and left apex cores). This is markedly elevated and consistent with high-grade, aggressive biology.

8. p63 / CK5/6 (basal cell cocktail): Used to confirm IDC-P versus invasive carcinoma in selected areas. IDC-P lesions retain a discontinuous peripheral basal cell layer (p63/CK5/6 positive), in contrast to invasive carcinoma (basal cells absent). Both patterns are present as described above.

9. Synaptophysin: NEGATIVE — no neuroendocrine differentiation identified.

10. Chromogranin A: NEGATIVE — no neuroendocrine differentiation identified.

MOLECULAR / GENOMIC STUDIES:
Tissue from this specimen is being submitted to an external reference laboratory for next-generation sequencing (FoundationOne CDx, Foundation Medicine). Results PENDING at time of this report. A separate addendum will be issued upon receipt.

Germline genetic testing from peripheral blood has also been ordered and is PENDING; results will be communicated through the Genetics/Genetic Counseling service separately.

FISH STUDIES: TMPRSS2-ERG fusion FISH not performed given negative ERG IHC; clinically not indicated at this time.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------
FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
PROSTATE, NEEDLE BIOPSY (12 CORES, TRUS-GUIDED):

ACINAR ADENOCARCINOMA, GRADE GROUP 5
  - Gleason Score: 5 + 4 = 9 (predominant pattern 5 with secondary pattern 4)
  - All 12 of 12 cores positive for carcinoma (100%)
  - Core involvement: 60% to 95% of core length
  - Perineural invasion: PRESENT (8 of 12 cores)
  - Lymphovascular invasion: PRESENT (suspicious/confirmed)
  - Intraductal carcinoma of the prostate (IDC-P): PRESENT, extensive (7 of 12 cores)
  - Neuroendocrine differentiation: NOT IDENTIFIED
  - PTEN expression by IHC: LOSS (heterogeneous)
  - ERG expression by IHC: NEGATIVE
  - AR expression: POSITIVE, strong, diffuse
  - Ki-67 proliferative index: ~35%

  NOTE: Molecular/NGS studies pending; addendum to follow.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATHOLOGIC STAGING (biopsy specimen only):
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
Pathologic T stage cannot be assigned on needle biopsy alone. Clinical staging per MRI and clinical examination: cT3b (bilateral seminal vesicle invasion), N1, M1b per treating team.

Findings are consistent with and support the clinical diagnosis of high-volume, high-grade, clinically metastatic prostate adenocarcinoma.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT:
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
This is a morphologically high-grade prostatic adenocarcinoma with multiple adverse pathologic features: Grade Group 5 (Gleason 9), 100% core positivity, extensive perineural invasion, suspicious lymphovascular invasion, and prominent intraductal carcinoma. The absence of neuroendocrine differentiation on the current specimen is noted; however, the high Ki-67 index (~35%) and extensive IDC-P component warrant close clinical follow-up for potential treatment-emergent neuroendocrine transformation if the patient is placed on prolonged androgen deprivation therapy. The PTEN loss by IHC is consistent with a clinically aggressive phenotype and may have implications for PI3K pathway-directed therapy in the future. ERG negativity by IHC is noted.

All immunohistochemical stains were performed with appropriate positive and negative controls.

Electronic sign-out by Attending Pathologist: [REDACTED]

Addendum (PENDING): Next-generation sequencing results to be reported separately upon receipt from external laboratory.
